description: Trigger discrimination for whole-stack cell (blast-radius) partitioning (this skill) vs per-component pooled/siloed isolation (saas-platform-architect), the architecture-style choice (architecture-advisor, whose menu omits cells), agent blast-radius (agent-containment-reviewer), and intra-cell write/size sharding (data-partitioning-sharding-strategist).

cases.prompt: Partition the platform so a failure or bad deploy is contained to a subset of tenants: each partition runs its own full stack, tenants map to exactly one partition, and we can roll changes out one partition at a time.
cases.note: SEAM CASE (primary) — a self-contained full-stack partition per tenant-subset, one-partition-at-a-time rollout, and containment of failure are the defining properties of cells, not a single-component isolation choice.
cases.reason: Whole-stack blast-radius partitioning with a thin router and cell-by-cell deploy is cell-based-architecture-designer. saas-platform-architect decides per-component pooled/siloed isolation, not full-stack cells.

cases.prompt: Decide the tenancy deployment model for our platform component by component — which stores are pooled with a tenant key, which are siloed per tenant, and what lives in the control plane vs the data plane.
cases.note: SEAM CASE (primary, reverse) — per-component pooled/siloed/bridge decisions and control/data-plane split are saas-platform-architect; cells are a heavier whole-stack partition on top of that.
cases.reason: Per-component tenancy isolation is saas-platform-architect. cell-based-architecture-designer partitions the WHOLE stack for blast radius, which is a different, scale-stage decision.

cases.prompt: We haven't picked an architecture paradigm yet — monolith, modular monolith, microservices, or event-driven. Recommend one for a small team building a CRUD-heavy product.
cases.reason: Choosing the architecture STYLE is architecture-advisor, whose style menu omits cells. Cells partition an already-chosen paradigm; they are not a paradigm being selected.

cases.prompt: In our multi-agent system one misbehaving agent can cascade failures across the whole agent network. Design blast-radius isolation, circuit breakers, and kill switches for the agents.
cases.reason: Blast radius across an AGENT network — cascade/rogue containment, circuit breakers, kill switches — is agent-containment-reviewer. cell-based-architecture-designer partitions INFRA, not agent authority.

cases.prompt: One tenant's write throughput and table size have outgrown a single node. We need to split that tenant's data across nodes with a shard key and a resharding plan.
cases.note: SEAM CASE (adjacency) — a tenant too big for one node is a write/size-scale sharding problem, not a fleet-partitioning one.
cases.reason: Splitting one tenant's data across nodes for write/size scale is data-partitioning-sharding-strategist. cell-based-architecture-designer partitions tenants across self-contained cells and assumes a tenant fits in one cell.
